tldr: Move developer/service secrets from 1Password vaults into AWS Secrets Manager so they live next to your AWS workloads, gated by IAM and rotated automatically.
why_switch: Native IAM-based access control instead of human-centric vault sharing
Built-in rotation for RDS, Redshift, DocumentDB, and custom Lambdas
Per-secret pay-as-you-go pricing rather than per-seat subscription
Tighter integration with AWS SDKs, ECS, EKS, and Lambda runtimes
Centralized audit trail via CloudTrail and KMS encryption keys you own
what_to_export: Vault and item names used to organize secrets
API keys, tokens, and OAuth client credentials
TLS certificates and private keys stored as documents
Access groups and who currently consumes each secret
export_method: Use the 1Password CLI (`op item list` / `op item get --format=json`) to dump items per vault, or export a vault to 1PUX/JSON from the desktop app. Script the CLI for repeatable, scoped exports rather than full-account dumps.
import_method: For each item, call `aws secretsmanager create-secret` (or `put-secret-value` on update) with the JSON payload. Group related fields into a single SecretString as JSON. Attach a KMS key, resource policy, and tags during creation; wire rotation Lambdas afterward where applicable.

concept_mapping.note: AWS has no vault primitive; emulate with `team/env/service` naming.
Pack username/password/token fields as JSON keys.
Used for cost allocation and ABAC.
Rotation moves stage labels; older versions retained.
Large blobs like certs may fit better in S3 with KMS.

effort: 1-3 days for <100 secrets; 1-2 weeks for org-wide rollout including IAM, rotation, and consumer updates.
gotchas: 1Password vault structure does not map 1:1; design a naming scheme before importing
Secrets Manager charges per secret per month plus per 10k API calls, so chatty consumers add cost
Rotation Lambdas must live in the same region as the secret
Deleted secrets enter a 7-30 day recovery window and keep billing during it
Cross-account access requires both IAM and resource policies; easy to misconfigure
Apps using the 1Password CLI/Connect need code changes to call the AWS SDK

email.subject: Migrating our secrets from 1Password to AWS Secrets Manager
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving service-level secrets from 1Password into AWS Secrets Manager so they sit next to the workloads that use them and can be rotated automatically. Plan: export the relevant vaults via the 1Password CLI, re-create each item as a JSON secret in AWS under a `team/env/service` naming scheme, attach IAM and KMS policies, then update apps to fetch via the AWS SDK. Human-only logins (browser passwords, personal accounts) stay in 1Password.

I'll share the naming convention and a cutover checklist before we start. Expect 1-2 weeks end-to-end.

Thanks
